author: Dr. Rebecca Yang (Neurology) / Dr. Marc Albertini (Neuropathology)
content:
MUSCLE BIOPSY — PROCEDURE NOTE AND HISTOPATHOLOGY REPORT
Date/Time: Procedure 3/19/2027, 10:00 AM; pathology finalized 3/22/2027, 1:30 PM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (MRN: MRN-90215734)
Procedure performed by: Dr. Rebecca Yang (Neuromuscular Neurology) with Dr. Joshua Kim (Interventional Radiology assistance for ultrasound guidance)
Pathologist of record: Dr. Marc Albertini (Neuropathology)

PROCEDURE: Open biopsy of right vastus lateralis muscle.
Indication: Workup of acute inflammatory myopathy with proximal weakness, elevated CK 2360-2580, EMG findings (3/18/2027) consistent with active inflammatory myopathy.
Location: Bedside in patient's hospital room (patient too unstable for OR transport).
Anesthesia: Local with 1% lidocaine.
Ultrasound guidance: Used to identify and avoid prominent vessels and to confirm muscle target with active inflammation (subtle hypoechoic changes noted).
Sample size: 2 specimens, approximately 1 × 0.5 × 0.5 cm each. One placed in glutaraldehyde for electron microscopy if needed; one snap-frozen in liquid nitrogen for routine and immunohistochemical processing.

PROCEDURE NOTE:
US-guided identification of right vastus lateralis. Skin prepped and draped. Lidocaine infiltrated. Small (3 cm) skin incision. Subcutaneous tissue divided; underlying fascia identified. Muscle visualized: pale, edematous appearance with subtle red discoloration in some fascicles. Two specimens excised. Hemostasis with electrocautery. Fascia closed with absorbable sutures. Skin closed with non-absorbable interrupted sutures (to be removed in 10-14 days). Sterile dressing.

Procedure tolerated well. No complications. Patient awake throughout with mother present. Procedure time 35 minutes. Estimated blood loss <5 mL.

Pathology specimens transported to neuropathology immediately.

---

HISTOPATHOLOGY REPORT — finalized 3/22/2027, 1:30 PM:

SPECIMEN: Right vastus lateralis muscle, biopsied 3/19/2027.

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Two fragments of skeletal muscle, each measuring 1.0 × 0.5 × 0.5 cm, tan-red with mild pallor and edema apparent. Specimen oriented for transverse cryosections.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
FROZEN SECTIONS — STANDARD STAINS:

H&E: Skeletal muscle showing a population of fibers with marked variation in fiber size. Numerous atrophic fibers, many of which are small and angulated, are scattered throughout. There are scattered ENDOMYSIAL MONONUCLEAR INFLAMMATORY INFILTRATES surrounding non-necrotic fibers. Several fibers show invasion by mononuclear cells (CD8+ T-cell pattern on immunohistochemistry). Multiple necrotic fibers with phagocytic cell infiltration. Notable PERIFASCICULAR ATROPHY in approximately 30% of fascicles examined — fibers at the periphery of fascicles are smaller and more angulated than central fibers. PERIVASCULAR INFLAMMATION at some endomysial and perimysial blood vessels.

NADH-TR: Internal architecture disorganized in many fibers. Patchy loss of mitochondrial staining. Some fibers show coarse moth-eaten changes.

Gomori trichrome (modified): No ragged red fibers. No tubular aggregates. No rimmed vacuoles (argues against inclusion body myositis).

ATPase (pH 9.4, pH 4.6, pH 4.3): Both type I and type II fibers affected. Type II atrophy not predominant (argues against pure steroid myopathy or disuse atrophy).

Acid phosphatase: Increased phagocytic activity in scattered necrotic fibers.

Oil Red O: Mild increase in lipid droplets in several fibers — non-specific.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
- CD8+ T cells: PROMINENT in endomysial inflammatory infiltrates; many CD8+ cells found invading non-necrotic muscle fibers.
- CD4+ T cells: Present in perivascular and perimysial areas.
- CD20+ B cells: Few, mostly perivascular.
- CD68 (macrophage marker): Increased in necrotic foci and scattered in interstitium.
- MHC class I (HLA-ABC): DIFFUSELY UPREGULATED on muscle fiber sarcolemma — present even on non-invaded fibers. Strong sarcolemmal expression.
- MHC class II: Increased focally.
- Membrane attack complex (C5b-9): Sarcolemmal deposition on a subset of fibers, predominantly in perifascicular regions.
- Dystrophin, dysferlin, sarcoglycans: Normal preservation (argues against muscular dystrophy).
- Caveolin, myotilin: Normal.

ELECTRON MICROSCOPY: Standard ultrastructural examination showed disorganization of myofibrils in many fibers with mitochondrial swelling. No tubuloreticular inclusions identified in endothelial cells (would have been more typical of dermatomyositis). No rimmed vacuoles. No nemaline rods or tubular aggregates.

DIAGNOSTIC INTERPRETATION:
The biopsy shows features of an ACTIVE INFLAMMATORY MYOPATHY with the following characteristics:

1. PROMINENT CD8+ T-CELL ENDOMYSIAL INFILTRATE invading non-necrotic muscle fibers — characteristic of POLYMYOSITIS / SLE-associated myositis pattern.

2. PERIFASCICULAR ATROPHY in ~30% of fascicles — a feature MORE CHARACTERISTIC OF DERMATOMYOSITIS but can be seen in SLE-myositis and in some immune-mediated necrotizing myopathies. Not 100% pathognomonic.

3. DIFFUSE MHC CLASS I UPREGULATION on muscle fiber sarcolemma — supports immune-mediated etiology and is characteristic of inflammatory myopathies in general.

4. C5b-9 sarcolemmal deposition in perifascicular regions — supports complement-mediated injury, characteristic of DERMATOMYOSITIS-like immune mechanism.

5. ABSENCE OF features that would suggest alternative diagnoses:
   - No rimmed vacuoles → not inclusion body myositis
   - Normal sarcoglycan/dystrophin → not muscular dystrophy
   - No ragged red fibers → not mitochondrial myopathy
   - No abundant necrosis with sparse inflammation → less consistent with pure necrotizing autoimmune myopathy
   - No tubuloreticular inclusions in endothelium → less classic for dermatomyositis but not exclusionary

OVERALL IMPRESSION: 
The biopsy demonstrates an active inflammatory myopathy with FEATURES OF BOTH polymyositis (CD8+ endomysial infiltrate invading non-necrotic fibers, diffuse MHC class I upregulation) AND dermatomyositis (perifascicular atrophy, C5b-9 deposition).

The biopsy provides a definitive diagnosis of an active immune-mediated inflammatory myopathy. The specific subclassification (pure SLE-myositis vs. SLE/DM overlap) will be further informed by the myositis-specific antibody (MSA) panel pending from 3/15/2027.

Clinical correlation strongly recommended. This pattern is highly TREATABLE with appropriate immunosuppression (corticosteroids, mycophenolate, methotrexate, with IVIG and/or rituximab for refractory cases).

NOTE: This is provisional final report. EM image library has been saved. Slides retained for further review if needed.
